expectations.text: Response does not contain actual implementation code
expectations.evidence: No code. Advisory text with pricing strategy discussion.

expectations.text: Lists at least 3 distinct risks or failure modes
expectations.evidence: 4 risks: revenue predictability loss, revenue loss from light users, sales simplicity loss, demand suppression (customers throttle usage to control costs).

expectations.text: Suggests at least 1 meaningfully different pricing approach
expectations.evidence: Proposes hybrid model: '$200/month base plus $0.01 per API call above a generous included tier.' Genuinely different from both pure per-seat and pure usage-based.

expectations.text: Includes at least 1 specific number, metric, or concrete data point
expectations.evidence: Uses $200/month and $0.01/call as illustrative hypotheticals, not researched data. No external benchmarks, industry stats, or real-world case studies cited. All numbers are the user's own ($50/seat) or made-up examples.

expectations.text: Poses questions back to the user to deepen discussion
expectations.evidence: 5 numbered questions: cost structure, revenue % from light users, heavy user alternatives, billing system readiness, sales team buy-in.

expectations.text: States a clear opinion rather than just listing pros/cons neutrally
expectations.evidence: Leans toward hybrid: 'Consider a hybrid model' and provides a phased migration path as a clear recommendation.

expectations.text: Makes clear this is an ongoing conversation, invites continued dialogue
expectations.evidence: Ends with general advice about companies that 'handle this transition well.' No direct invitation to continue the conversation or challenge the recommendation.
